free_text: A multi-parameter study of LRA fission and binary
Well-collimated thermal neutron flux at sample
diameter, on a backing of 50 ug/cm2 and 40 mm diameter.
Two (F1 and F2) ORTEC silicon surface barrier
detectors each of 24 mm diameter - for fission
Two (alpha1 and alpha2) groups of 5cm x 1 cm
rectangular surface barrier detectors each consisting
of 4 detectors arranged in semi-cylindrical form - for
LRA fission was measured about 4 weeks.
Each day 100000 binary fission events were recorded.
energy of two fission fragments El and Eh,

procedure to convert pulse height into kinetic energy
Random coincidence rate < 2% of the true coincidence
Measured masses are pseudo-masses, they were not
corrected for neutron emission. Neutron emission do not
influence the pseudo-masses very differently since
nubar is not very different (1.8 for ternary, 2.45 for
Additional effect of recoil caused by the
alpha-particle emission at ternary fission was not
taken into account in calculation of ternary fission
Measured energy distributions were fitted by Gaussians
